body:
HEADLINE FINDING (found in OnInit, but it's systemic across the whole file -- flagging immediately rather than waiting for a batch report): the ComponentWeights struct (line 1402) has only 6 fields now -- mtf, momentum, volatility, volume, session, pattern. At some point it was consolidated down from what used to be ~9-10 separate concepts (trend, momentum, directional, reversal, volatility, volume, session, pattern, predictive). Confirmed via '/* was: X */' comments left in the code -- 'directional' got merged into 'momentum', 'reversal' got merged into 'volatility', 'predictive' got merged into 'pattern', 'trend' got merged into 'mtf'.

The problem: the struct rename happened, but the SURROUNDING CODE never got updated to match, at dozens of call sites throughout the entire file (OnInit init, save-to-file, load-from-file, backtested-discovery apply, SmartCB circuit-breaker save/restore, live adaptive weight tuning -- I found it at 14194, 20190-20248, 20628-20642, 22649-22826, 23257-23791, 25854-25859, 26590-27123, 28071-28077, 34971, 45266-45271). Every one of these sites still writes (or reads) BOTH of the original pre-merge values into what is now the SAME single field, one right after the other -- e.g.:
  g_ComponentWeights.volatility = <reversal value>;
  g_ComponentWeights.volatility = <volatility value>;   // silently overwrites the line above
This isn't dead code -- it's actively discarding real data every time it runs, because g_Discovery.bestReversalWeight, g_SmartCB.lastOptimalReversalWeight, and g_HistoricalBacktester.topConfig1.reversalWeight all still exist as genuinely separate, independently-computed values elsewhere in the file (they wouldn't have distinct names and distinct backtested results if they were meant to be identical to their volatility/momentum/pattern counterparts). So real optimization/discovery results for reversal weight, directional weight, and predictive weight are computed correctly, then silently thrown away every time they're saved, loaded, or applied, because they land in a field that's about to be (or was just) overwritten by a different concept.

This also corrupts the file save/load round-trip specifically: SaveOptimalWeights (~23257) writes 6 doubles to disk in a sequence that maps to only 4 distinct live fields, and the loader (~23786) reads them back in the same colliding order -- so persisted calibration state for reversal/directional/predictive weights doesn't survive a restart correctly even if the in-memory values were briefly right.

This is the single highest-value fix I've found across the whole review so far -- it affects weight calibration, live self-tuning, backtesting/discovery, and disk persistence simultaneously, all from one root cause. Real fix requires a decision from you: either (a) restore mtf/momentum/volatility/volume/session/pattern PLUS separate reversal/directional/predictive fields on the struct (6 -> 9 fields) and fix every call site to target the correct one, or (b) if the original consolidation was intentional (e.g. you decided reversal and volatility really should be the same tunable weight going forward), then the SURROUNDING code should be cleaned up to stop computing/tracking bestReversalWeight etc. as if they were still separate. I don't know which was the original intent, so I'm not picking one and quietly fixing it -- this changes what the system's weight-tuning is actually optimizing for, which is your call, not a bug-fix judgment call.

Continuing through the rest of OnInit in the meantime.
